title: PRIMARY KEY expression in column declaration does not work with COMMENT
description:
### Company or project name
Company: ClickHouse
Project: sqitchers/sqitch
### Describe the unexpected behaviour
An inline `PRIMARY KEY` declaration works:
``` SQL
CREATE TABLE releases (
version REAL PRIMARY KEY
) ENGINE = MergeTree;
```
As does an inline `COMMENT` with a PK after the table declaration:
``` SQL
CREATE TABLE releases (
version REAL COMMENT 'Version of the registry.'
) ENGINE = MergeTree PRIMARY KEY(version);
```
But it does not work to inline both `PRIMARY KEY` and `COMMENT`:
``` SQL
CREATE TABLE releases (
version REAL PRIMARY KEY COMMENT 'Version of the registry.'
) ENGINE = MergeTree;
```
The error:
```
Syntax error: failed at position 54 (COMMENT) (line 2, col 30):
CREATE TABLE releases (
version REAL PRIMARY KEY COMMENT 'Version of the registry.'
) ENGINE = MergeTree;
Expected one of: SETTINGS, token, Comma, ClosingRoundBracket
```
What does work, unexpectedly (to me), is to put the `COMMENT` _before_ the `PRIMARY KEY`:
``` SQL
CREATE TABLE releases (
version REAL COMMENT 'Version of the registry.' PRIMARY KEY
) ENGINE = MergeTree;
```
I guess there is an order required, though I don't see why. It also deviates from how other database engines support comment syntax. Here's MySQL, for example:
``` SQL
CREATE TABLE releases (
version         FLOAT(4, 1)   PRIMARY KEY
COMMENT 'Version of the registry.'
) ENGINE  InnoDB,
CHARACTER SET 'utf8',
COMMENT 'Sqitch registry releases.'
;
```
### Which ClickHouse versions are affected?
I'm running 25.9.1.1112.
### How to reproduce
Fire up Clickhouse and run
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE releases (version FLOAT COMMENT 'Version of the registry.' PRIMARY KEY);
CREATE TABLE releases_v2 (version FLOAT COMMENT 'Version of the registry.');
CREATE TABLE releases_v3 (id INTEGER, version FLOAT, description VARCHAR(255) COMMENT 'Description of the release.' PRIMARY KEY(id));
INSERT INTO releases_v3 (id, version, description) VALUES (1, 1.0, 'Initial release');
key_bug_features: Inline PRIMARY KEY and COMMENT in column declaration
